summary: Backend tests for P1 tasks: (1) Page Blocks admin CRUD + bulk /reorder + seed-default-home, and (2) MNG Kargo public webhook /api/orders/cargo/mng-webhook. All test cases passed against the public preview URL. 12/13 passed; 1 skipped (no admin HTTP GET endpoint for integration_logs, but underlying Mongo write was independently verified — 8 mng_kargo logs persisted correctly).

minor.issue: No admin HTTP endpoint to read integration_logs (Mongo write is correct). Considering adding an admin viewer (already covered indirectly by webhook log persistence verified via Mongo).

action_items: (Optional) Expose GET /api/integration-logs admin endpoint to surface webhook history in admin UI; collection 'integration_logs' is being populated correctly.
(Optional) Add explicit Pydantic models for /page-blocks payloads (currently dict-typed) to enforce field validation.
critical_code_review_comments: cms.py PUT /page-blocks/{id} accepts arbitrary dict — caller could overwrite 'id' or 'created_at' fields. Recommend whitelisting allowed keys (type/title/images/links/settings/page/sort_order/is_active).
cms.py POST /page-blocks/reorder loops update_one per id — for 5 blocks it's fine, but consider bulk_write/UpdateMany for larger pages.

orders.py mng_cargo_webhook: 'cargo_tracking' field is queried in admin GET filter (line 48) but webhook stores barkod in 'cargo_tracking_number'; admin search filter uses different key. Field naming inconsistency could surface elsewhere.

orders.py mng_cargo_webhook: ISLEM_KODU '200' currently maps to 'shipped' along with '300'. MNG spec calls 200 'Transfere alındı' (in transit at hub) — semantically not yet 'shipped (out for delivery)'. Acceptable but worth documenting.
orders.py PUT /orders/{order_id} also accepts arbitrary dict (same risk as cms PUT) — admin endpoint, lower risk but still recommend whitelisting.

success_rate.backend: 100% (12/12 executed; 1 skipped due to missing admin reader endpoint, not a bug)

test_credentials: Admin: admin@facette.com / admin123 via POST /api/auth/login query params
seed_data_creation: test_seed_default_home_overwrite seeded 5 default home page_blocks (hero_slider, full_banner, half_banners, product_slider, instashop). CRUD/reorder tests created and cleaned up TEST_BLOCK_* blocks. MNG webhook fixture mutated 1 existing order's status (shipped→delivered→shipped via 200) and tracking number; teardown attempts to restore original status/tracking via PUT /api/orders/{id}.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Test file: /app/backend/tests/test_iteration24_pageblocks_mng_webhook.py covers /api/page-blocks (GET list sort, GET by id, POST create, PUT update, DELETE, POST /reorder bulk, POST /seed-default-home overwrite & idempotency, admin auth enforcement) and /api/orders/cargo/mng-webhook (public no-auth, 400 on empty BARKOD/REFERANS_NO, unknown barkod silent log, BARKOD match → 300 shipped, 400 delivered + delivered_at, REFERANS_NO match by order_number → 200 shipped). Admin order list endpoint is GET /api/orders (NOT /api/admin/orders). Order update is PUT /api/orders/{id} accepting dict. integration_logs collection write verified via direct Mongo query (8 mng_kargo entries). status_map: 100→preparing, 200/300→shipped, 400→delivered (+delivered_at set from TARIH), 500→returned. Webhook fixture picks first order from list, sets cargo_tracking_number to TEST*, and restores original status+tracking on teardown — be aware this temporarily mutates production-looking data; safe rollback included.
rca of the issue: N/A — no failing tests. Initial run had 4 skips because fixture used /api/admin/orders (404 → no such route) and PATCH (405 → only PUT supported). Fixed by switching to GET /api/orders + PUT /api/orders/{id}; now all 4 webhook tests pass.
